company_box_content: 关于舍弗勒 舍弗勒集团75余年来始终秉承开拓创新精神，致力于推动驱动技术的创新与发展。依托在电驱动、低碳驱动、底盘应用和可再生能源领域提供创新技术、产品和服务，舍弗勒集团致力于成为值得信赖的合作伙伴，让驱动技术在整个产品生命周期中更高效、更智能、更可持续。舍弗勒集团的产品和服务覆盖整个驱动技术生态系统，涉及八大产品领域，包括从轴承解决方案、各类直线导轨系统到维修和监测服务等丰富的产品和服务组合。舍弗勒集团目前拥有约110,000名员工，在全球55个国家和地区设有250多个分支机构，是全球最大家族企业之一，也是德国最具创新力公司之一。
company_description: Schaeffler is a dynamic global technology company with a history of private ownership, partnering with major automobile manufacturers and key players in aerospace and industrial sectors. It employs about 110,000 people across 55 countries and 250+ branches, focusing on innovation in e-drive, low-carbon drive, chassis applications, and renewable energy.

head: BI Analyst (Supply Analytics, Bangkok-based, Relocation provided)
info: 中国 广东省 深圳 · 重新发布时间: 3 天前 · 7 位会员点击过“申请”由招聘者推广 ·
job_details_content: ##              职位简介
About Agoda

At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world.

Today, we are part of Booking Holdings [NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. Every day, we connect people to destinations and experiences, with our great deals across our millions of hotels and holiday properties, flights, and experiences worldwide.

No two days are the same at Agoda. Data and technology are at the heart of our culture, fueling our curiosity and innovation. If you’re ready to begin your best journey and help build travel for the world, join us.
THIS ROLE IS BASED IN BANGKOK, THAILAND (WITH RELOCATION PROVIDED)
Location: Bangkok, Thailand (*Not open for remote work)
Get to Know our Team:

As part of the Supply Analytics, you will be responsible for identifying new partners and opportunities, cultivating a relationship with them, and working together with them to deliver the best outcome for our end customers. You would also be responsible with managing the day-to-day interaction with the partner and working closely with other teams within Agoda to ensure the partners needs are met.

We design tailored business and product solutions with our partners and help them generate measurable value. Members of our team are empowered and supported to grow their market(s) or accounts. We develop win-win relationships and leverage Agoda’s unique accommodations portfolio and tech solutions to bring our partners the advantages they seek. Utilizing our strong brand and resources, we build new channels to increase the visibility of Agoda, introduce more travelers to our great products and service and deliver significant revenues to the overall business.
The Opportunity:

As a BI Analyst, you will report directly to either the Senior Manager or Associate Director within the Supply Department and this will be an individual contributor role. You will be responsible and fully empowered to work with the partners on the ground. You will be supported by a team within the Supply department and work closely with other Team members within Agoda.
In this Role, you’ll get to
Translate internal briefs into analytical projects (to include refining the initial brief and asking the ‘right questions’, working through potential hypotheses and storyboarding the output)
Use and analyze data from multiple large-scale data warehouses and present statistically strong analysis to a wide range of business stakeholders.
Proactively identify opportunities for growth within supply and the wider business.
Drive new analytical initiatives and projects aimed at improving organizational efficiency and shaping Agoda supply.
Identify, support, and lead projects aimed at scaling up the way the Supply organization leverages on data, insights, and intelligence.
Automate manual operational processes and present back on time savings gained through modernization of business operations
What you’ll Need to Succeed
At least 2-5+ years of experience working as an Analyst with experience in analytics/data science/insights/strategy/BI
Advanced working knowledge and hands-on experience in SQL
Strong knowledge and hands-on experience in data visualization tools such as Tableau (preferably)
Expert domain of data analysis and data visualization tools and software such as Excel, Python (or R)
Bachelor’s degree ideally in a business or quantitative subject (e.g. computer science, mathematics, engineering, science, economics or finance).
A good understanding of statistical modelling knowledge or any machine learning technique knowledge (such as hypothesis testing, regression, logistic regression, random forest, etc.)
Good stakeholder management experience. Comfortable presenting to senior leadership and C-suite.
Experience in conducting A/B testing experimentation
Strong experience in finding data insights and provide business recommendation to the business

A hacker’s mindset – the ability to build simple but clever and elegant solutions to new problems within significant resource, operational and time constraints through deep understanding of the business, creative problem solving, and a wide range of expertise in data, analytics, automation, programming, and prototyping.
Excellent communicator with superior written, verbal, presentation and interpersonal communication skills.
Data driven in both decision making and performance measurement.
Extreme comfort in ambiguous, fast-paced environment.
Ability to multi-task, prioritize and coordinate resources.
It’s Great if you Have:
MBA or Masters in a quantitative subject (e.g. computer science, mathematics, engineering, science, economics or finance).
Program management certifications (e.g. PMI, PRINCE2) to compliment your program management experience.
Asian market experience.
Travel industry / e-commerce / tech / consulting experience.

We do so by enabling more people to see the world for less – with our best-value deals across our 6,000,000+ hotels and holiday properties, 130,000+ flight routes, 360,000+ activities, and more.

Agoda was founded in 2005 in Thailand by two lifelong friends with a shared passion for travel. Today, Agoda is part of Booking Holdings [BKNG], and we have more than 7,000 employees from 90 nationalities in offices across Asia Pacific, the Middle East, Europe, and the Americas.

In every department – from engineering to customer support – you’ll find that data and technology are at the heart of our culture. There’s never a boring day at Agoda as we aim to make travel hassle-free for everyone. If you’re ready to begin your best journey with us and help us build travel for the world, join us.

head: Cost Management Analyst
info: 广州-佛山地区 · 重新发布时间: 1 天前 · 10 位会员点击过“申请”由招聘者推广 ·
job_details_content: ##              职位简介
Some careers have more impact than others.
If you’re looking for a career where you can make a real impression, join HSBC and discover how valued you’ll be.

HSBC is one of the largest banking and financial services organisations in the world, with operations in 64 countries and territories. We aim to be where the growth is, enabling businesses to thrive and economies to prosper, and, ultimately, helping people to fulfil their hopes and realise their ambitions.
We are currently seeking an experienced professional to join our team in the role of AVP - COST MANAGEMENT ANALYST.
Principal responsibilities
Develop new datasets which link customer demographics to operational costs and revenues to support customer-centric business decisioning regarding cost reduction initiatives.
Support development of Total Cost of Ownership models which allow management to make fact-based business decisions with full visibility of the impact on customers and revenue.

job_details_content: ##              职位简介
Responsibilities

Budget formulation and rolling forecast: Develop the annual budget based on sales volume and industry competition predictions of the business. Monitor monthly forecasts based on the actual sales delivery and expenses continuously to identify risks and opportunities of the operation.

Policy Analysis and Budget Control: Woking with sales and marketing, analyze the policy to make sure the effectiveness, feasibility and controlled within budget and provide financial support and advice to business teams. Review business policy announcements to ensure consistency with approved policy and operability.

Marketing Expense Reimbursement: Review the sales volume submitted by dealers and the calculation of variable marketing expenses based on the announced marketing policy to ensure that the payment consistent with the proposal and announcement.

Variable Marketing Expense Closing: Conduct accounting of variable marketing expenses based on the actual sales volume and released policy. Compared with the monthly forecast, analyze the variance of business changes to optimize it continuously.

Standardization and Dealer Support: Regularly review and optimize the variable marketing expense process to make sure the standardized operations. Support the special reports of dealers and the resolution of the problems in process.

head: Senior/Market Analyst, Petcoke/Anode
info: 中国 北京市 北京市 · 2 周前 · 14 位会员点击过“申请”由招聘者推广 ·
job_details_content: ##              职位简介
Argus is where smart people belong and where they can grow. We answer the challenge of illuminating markets and shaping new futures.
What will you be doing?
We are looking for a driven, passionate, and resourceful
market analyst

to join our team to cover China’s fast-growing petroleum coke and thermal coal markets. The successful candidate will join our global network of over 600 dedicated commodity market analysts, to deliver expert market analyses and information. We pride ourselves on analysing how and why news matter to our high-level audience, which comprises Fortune 500 companies, national oil and energy companies, multinationals, financial institutions, trading companies, and governments.
In this role, you will cover the
China petroleum coke, battery anode materials and other relevant markets

deciding price assessments as well as writing analyses and news stories. You will maintain and expand the contact base, and you will represent Argus at industry events. The analyst will also spearhead development of market coverage alongside their line managers.

You will write engaging market intelligence and provide analysis of spot prices that are timely, accurate, objective, and meaningful for our clients. This includes maintaining a strong network of contacts within the industry to source original, relevant and exclusive content. You will need to be personable and be able to quickly become a subject matter expert and lead coverage within the designated product portfolio. You will enjoy collaborative work with colleagues from various backgrounds, many located in Argus’ other offices around the globe. In addition, there are opportunities to travel, attend industry events, present at industry conferences and lead webinar sessions.

This is an excellent opportunity for market analysts, consultants, or brokers to transfer their skills and knowledge to advance their careers by joining a growing, global, independently owned organisation.
Monitor and assess fast-moving markets to produce daily price assessments, market commentaries, analyses, and reports.
Establish strong relationships with key members of the industry and business leaders through meetings and daily communications to gain an in-depth understanding of your market.

Proactively generate pitch stories, contribute copy to the relevant publication and the online publication service, and contribute to unique content (blog posts, white papers, webinars, podcasts, etc.).
Collaborate with colleagues on the desk and other teams to enhance and drive coverage.
Attend meetings, conferences, and industry events to build contacts and represent Argus in the industry.
Build your industry expertise and writing skills to provide readers with first-class market insights and analysis.
To learn more about the report this role covers, please click on the below link:
Power Generation Industry: Market Insights & Trends | Argus Media
What We’re Looking For In You
A Bachelor’s degree in economics, English, communications, business, statistics or a relevant field.
Prior experience in the financial, energy, commodities or market analysis & consulting sector is beneficial but not essential.
Excellent writing, grammar, and numeracy skills.
Demonstrable interest in global commodity markets.
Strong co-ordination and time management skills
Excellent command of spoken and written English and Chinese.
Strong experience with Microsoft Office Suite with experience in Excel.
A commitment to the highest level of accuracy and completing all writing within established deadlines.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to network and build contacts with industry executives.
Able to work independently while engaging and collaborating in line with the Argus values: Excellence, Integrity, Partnership, and Inclusivity.
What’s In It For You
Work within a highly motivated team with an industry leader and rapidly growing global company.
Competitive salary and bonus structure.
Hybrid working model. Work from Home options available
Career development and progression opportunities.
15 days annual leave.
Group healthcare scheme.
Career development and promotion from within culture.
Ongoing training, mentoring and development opportunities.
Employee Welfare & Employee Assistance Programme.
Travel
Business meetings, cover conferences and events (5-10% of domestic/overseas travel is expected).
Hours
Monday through Friday, 9.00 a.m. to 6.00 p.m.
Earlier or later hours, or weekend hours, may be expected on occasion.

Argus is the leading independent provider of market intelligence to the global energy and commodity markets. We offer essential price assessments, news, analytics, consulting services, data science tools and industry conferences to illuminate complex and opaque commodity markets.
Headquartered in London with 1,500 staff, Argus is an independent media organisation with 30 offices in the world’s principal commodity trading hubs.

Companies, trading firms and governments in 160 countries around the world trust Argus data to make decisions, analyse situations, manage risk, facilitate trading and for long-term planning. Argus prices are used as trusted benchmarks around the world for pricing transportation, commodities and energy.
Founded in 1970, Argus remains a privately held UK-registered company owned by employee shareholders and global growth equity firm General Atlantic.
1. Monitor and assess fast-moving markets to produce daily price assessments, market commentaries, analyses, and reports.
2. Establish strong relationships with key members of the industry and business leaders through meetings and daily communications to gain an in-depth understanding of your market.

3. Proactively generate pitch stories, contribute copy to the relevant publication and the online publication service, and contribute to unique content (blog posts, white papers, webinars, podcasts, etc.).
4. Collaborate with colleagues on the desk and other teams to enhance and drive coverage.
5. Attend meetings, conferences, and industry events to build contacts and represent Argus in the industry.
6. Build your industry expertise and writing skills to provide readers with first-class market insights and analysis.
1. A Bachelor’s degree in economics, English, communications, business, statistics or a relevant field.
2.  Prior experience in the financial, energy, commodities or market analysis & consulting sector is beneficial but not essential.
3. Excellent writing, grammar, and numeracy skills.
4. Demonstrable interest in global commodity markets.
5. Strong co-ordination and time management skills
6. Excellent command of spoken and written English and Chinese.
7. Strong experience with Microsoft Office Suite with experience in Excel.
8. A commitment to the highest level of accuracy and completing all writing within established deadlines.
9. Excellent communication skills with the ability to network and build contacts with industry executives.
10. Able to work independently while engaging and collaborating in line with the Argus values: Excellence, Integrity, Partnership, and Inclusivity.
1. Work within a highly motivated team with an industry leader and rapidly growing global company.
2. Competitive salary and bonus structure.
3. Hybrid working model. Work from Home options available
4. Career development and progression opportunities.
5. 15 days annual leave.
6. Group healthcare scheme.
7. Career development and promotion from within culture.
8. Ongoing training, mentoring and development opportunities.
9. Employee Welfare & Employee Assistance Programme.
1. Business meetings, cover conferences and events (5-10% of domestic/overseas travel is expected).
1. Monday through Friday, 9.00 a.m. to 6.00 p.m.
2. Earlier or later hours, or weekend hours, may be expected on occasion.
company_box_content: Argus is the leading independent provider of market intelligence to the global energy and commodity markets. We offer essential price assessments, news, analytics, consulting services, data science tools and industry conferences to illuminate complex and opaque commodity markets.
Headquartered in London with over 1,500 staff, Argus is an independent media organisation with 30 offices in the world’s principal commodity trading hubs.

Companies, trading firms and governments in 160 countries around the world trust Argus data to make decisions, analyse situations, manage risk, facilitate trading and for long-term planning. Argus prices are used as trusted benchmarks around the world for pricing transportation, commodities and energy.
Founded in 1970, Argus remains a privately held UK-registered company owned by employee shareholders and global growth equity firm General Atlantic.
company_description: Argus Media is a leading independent provider of market intelligence for global energy and commodity markets, with over 1,500 staff in 30 offices worldwide. It offers price assessments, news, analytics, consulting, and data science tools, trusted by companies, trading firms, and governments in 160 countries for decision-making and risk management.

head: Actuarial Analyst
info: 中国 上海市 上海市 · 2 周前 · 20 位会员点击过“申请”由招聘者推广 ·
job_details_content: ##              职位简介
Shape Your Future With Us

General Re Corporation, a subsidiary of Berkshire Hathaway Inc., is a holding company for global reinsurance and related operations, with more than 2,000 employees worldwide. It owns General Reinsurance Corporation and General Reinsurance AG, which conducts business as Gen Re.

Gen Re delivers reinsurance solutions to the Life/Health and Property/Casualty insurance industries. Represented in all major reinsurance markets through a network of 37 offices, we have earned superior financial strength ratings from each of the major rating agencies. We value diversity, equality and inclusion and promote a culture that supports professional and personal growth.
Your Role

Our Corporate Actuarial Service Life/Health Unit is seeking to appoint a dynamic individual as Actuarial Analyst to work in our Shanghai office. In this role, you will support the reserving activities as well as actuarial and financial reporting for Life/Health business in China.
Your Key Responsibilities
1. Support the actuarial reporting under local statutory/IFRS bases and solvency regime
2. Support the reserve calculation (e.g., Mathematical Reserve/IBNR) under German GAAP
3. Support AXIS modelling and maintenance of AXIS model for local statutory (CROSS) and IFRS 17 reporting
4. Support the IFRS 17 implementation in China
5. Conduct, review or validate experience studies to develop reserving bases and best estimate bases
6. Participate in regional and international initiatives and projects
Your Profile
1. A near qualified Actuary with 2 - 5 years of relevant work experience in a life insurance or reinsurance company in China
2. Working knowledge of statutory or local GAAP reporting requirements in China
3. Solid knowledge and prior experience in AXIS or other actuarial software (e.g., Prophet)
4. Proficient in coding (e.g., Python or R), database (e.g.MS Access, SQL) and reporting tools (e.g., Power BI)
5. Good command of Chinese and English, both spoken and written
6. Strong attention to detail and sense of accountability
7. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
8. Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities
We offer a
1. highly professional and inclusive work environment that values excellence, integrity, personal responsibility and the unique talent of each employee
2. flat management hierarchy and team-based environment that promote collaboration and free-flowing communication
3. market competitive remuneration package commensurate with experience and qualifications, with a comprehensive suite of employee benefits
Does this sound exciting?
If you want to shape your future with us, we look forward to receiving your application via the Apply Now link.
Our Address
General Reinsurance AG
Shanghai Branch
Unit 021, Level 20, Hang Seng Bank Tower
1000 Lujiazui Ring Road
Shanghai 200120 (People's Republic of China)
company_box_content: Gen Re is a leading property/casualty and life/health reinsurance company. We are owned by General Re Corporation, a reinsurance holding company for global reinsurance and related operations and a subsidiary of Berkshire Hathaway Inc.

Gen Re delivers Treaty and Facultative reinsurance solutions to companies in all segments of the insurance industry. Our direct business model ensures our clients have direct access to decision makers, and underwriting, actuarial and claims experts, who understand and support their business objectives.
Gen Re is headquartered in Stamford, Connecticut. A network of 37 branch and subsidiary offices are located globally in key markets.

Our professionals bring a combination of local knowledge and global strength to our clients. We work closely with our clients to understand their objectives so that we can provide relevant capacity and differentiated services to them.

Gen Re hires and trains the best underwriters, actuaries and claims professionals in the industry. We are in the risk assumption business - just like our clients - so the work that we do on a daily basis is a valuable component of how we see and evaluate risk. We share this experience with our clients in various ways - via direct underwriter-to-underwriter dialogue, research, technical seminars, underwriting and claims roundtables and audits, to name a few.
For more information about Gen Re and access to all our current publications, please visit us on www.genre.com.
company_description: Gen Re is a leading reinsurance company owned by General Re Corporation, a subsidiary of Berkshire Hathaway Inc., with over 2,000 employees globally. It provides treaty and facultative reinsurance solutions for property/casualty and life/health insurance, operating through 37 branch and subsidiary offices with expertise in underwriting, actuarial, and claims services.

head: Technical Risk Analyst
info: 中国 上海市 上海市 · 2 周前 · 21 位会员点击过“申请”由招聘者推广 ·
job_details_content: ##              职位简介

Since its establishment in 2012, our Shanghai office is a rapidly growing participant in the Chinese markets, trading exchange-listed futures, options and equities in China mainland. Our vision is to become the trusted partner in the development of Chinese financial markets. With the culture of a start-up but the backing of a 35+ year-old trading firm, the Optiver Shanghai office is truly unique. Everyone who joins us will help shape the future of our company and its global impact. Get ready: we are only just beginning.
About The Role

As a Technical Risk Analyst within Tech Risk and Enablement (TRE), you will design frameworks that enable risk management at scale and lead initiatives to identify emerging risks across technology, including those introduced by new AI tools and platforms. Partnering with subject matter experts, you will assess control effectiveness and help implement practical solutions that ensure risks are managed effectively and remain within the firm’s appetite.

You’ll play a central role in helping technology functions identify, own, and manage risk effectively, balancing risk and reward to achieve operational excellence while supporting innovation. Acting as a bridge between engineering teams and senior technology leadership (CTOs), you will provide regular risk reporting and insights that inform decision-making. This feedback loop will give you the context to influence teams on the ground and ensure alignment between strategy and execution.

This is a unique opportunity to shape how technology manages risk in APAC, gaining exposure to both strategic leadership and day-to-day engineering operations. Your work will have a visible impact on the resilience, efficiency, and continued growth of Optiver’s technology function.
What You’ll Do
Support technology teams in managing risks in line with our risk appetite.
Provide clear risk reporting and insights to senior management, strengthening decision-making.
Facilitate incident reviews, process deep dives, and cross-team projects to drive improvements.
Surface emerging risks and recurring themes, helping teams address them proactively.
Collaborate with risk and control functions to strengthen frameworks and consistency.
Who You Are
5+ years’ experience in technology risk, IT operations, or operational risk management.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.
Comfortable engaging with senior leaders while influencing engineering teams.
Pragmatic, collaborative, and resilient in a fast-paced environment.
What you will get
The chance to work alongside diverse and intelligent peers in a rewarding environment.
Competitive remuneration, including an attractive bonus structure
Training, mentorship and personal development opportunities.
Gym membership plus weekly in-house chair massages.
Daily breakfast, lunch and an in-house barista.

As an intentionally flat organisation, we believe that great ideas and impact can come from everyone. We are passionate about empowering individuals and creating diverse teams that thrive. Every person at Optiver should feel included, valued, and respected, because we believe our best work is done together.

Our commitment to diversity and inclusion is hardwired through every stage of our hiring process. We encourage applications from candidates from all backgrounds, and we welcome requests for reasonable adjustments during the process to ensure that you can best demonstrate your abilities.
Optiver is committed to
diversity and inclusion
. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds, and welcome requests for reasonable adjustments during the process.

company_box_content: Create better access and prices for all – that’s our goal for financial markets the world over. As a leading global electronic market maker, we are dedicated to our mission of continuously improving the market. It’s a responsibility we don’t take lightly. It’s the driving force behind our business. Even in times of extreme volatility, we stay the course. Resolute in our belief that the market should always be liquid for economies to thrive.

As one of the oldest market-making institutions, we’re a trusted partner of exchanges. Our contribution to them ensures they remain stable, transparent and competitive. We provide liquidity to financial markets using our own capital, at our own risk, trading a wide range of products: listed derivatives, cash equities, ETFs, bonds, and foreign currencies.
We strongly believe in the positive power of the world’s markets. That’s why we’re committed to leading the way in market making for environmental, social, and corporate governance (ESG) products.
Optiver is a global company with offices in Amsterdam, Sydney, Chicago, Austin, Taipei, Hong Kong, Shanghai, London and Singapore.
company_description: Optiver is a global electronic market maker dedicated to improving financial markets by providing liquidity and stable, transparent trading. With offices in key locations like Amsterdam, Sydney, and Shanghai, it trades listed derivatives, equities, ETFs, bonds, and currencies, focusing on ESG products and trusted partnership with exchanges.

head: AI Engineer
info: 中国 北京市 · 2 周前 · 54 位申请者由招聘者推广 · 积极审核申请者
job_details_content: ##              职位简介
岗位职责
1. 参与设计并实现基于大模型的AI应用方案；
2. 参与构建与优化 RAG（Retrieval-Augmented Generation）系统，协助数据检索、模型调优与性能优化；
3. 学习并掌握 Transformer 架构及主流预训练模型（如BERT、Llama、GPT等），探索其在不同业务场景中的应用；
4. 负责数据集的收集、清洗与标注工作，支持模型训练与测试实验，并参与基于实验反馈的模型改进；
5. 与跨职能团队（产品、算法、工程）协作，支持AI模型的API部署与产品功能落地；
6. 持续关注 Web3、区块链与AI结合方向 的最新研究进展，参与创新应用探索项目。
任职要求
1. 计算机科学、人工智能、机器学习、数据科学或相关专业，本科及以上学历；
2. 具备扎实的编程基础，熟悉 Python / Java / Go 等至少一种语言；
3. 了解 Transformer 模型原理，对LLM、大语言模型或RAG系统有基础理解；
4. 熟悉数据处理与分析流程，如数据预处理、ETL、SQL、Pandas、向量数据库等；
5. 了解深度学习框架（PyTorch / TensorFlow）；
6. 具备良好的学习能力、逻辑思维与问题解决能力，能主动探索AI与业务结合的创新应用；
7. 有以下经历者优先：
1. 曾参与RAG或大模型微调项目（课程、科研、实习均可）；
2. 了解Web3 / 区块链基础知识；
3. 在AI竞赛、开源项目中有贡献记录（如GitHub、Kaggle）

head: Research Analyst, Metals & Mining
info: 中国 北京市 北京市 · 2 周前 · 45 位会员点击过“申请”由招聘者推广 ·
job_details_content: ##              职位简介

Wood Mackenzie is the global data and analytics business for the renewables, energy, and natural resources industries. Enhanced by technology. Enriched by human intelligence. In an ever-changing world, companies and governments need reliable and actionable insight to lead the transition to a sustainable future. That’s why we cover the entire supply chain with unparalleled breadth and depth, backed by over 50 years’ experience. Our team of over 2,400 experts, operating across 30 global locations, are enabling customers’ decisions through real-time analytics, consultancy, events and thought leadership. Together, we deliver the insight they need to separate risk from opportunity and make confident decisions when it matters most.
WoodMac.com
Wood Mackenzie Brand Video
Wood Mackenzie Values
Inclusive – we succeed together
Trusting – we choose to trust each other
Customer committed – we put customers at the heart of our decisions
Future Focused – we accelerate change
Curious – we turn knowledge into action
Role Purpose

We monitor key events and conduct research to produce thought-provoking reports, data models, and presentations. From asset valuation to corporate reports, from market supply-demand research to price-forecasting, and from the regional to the global, our analysis helps shape our customers’ commercial strategies.

We also support bespoke consulting projects, diving deeper into our data to answer unique questions. We help our Sales team retain and win new business by spending time with customers and answering questions that matter to them. We work closely with colleagues in our Data and Product functions to maintain the quality of our published data and to deliver content via our
growing Lens platform

Through carrying out primary research, responding to customer questions, and presenting to industry stakeholders, you will build relationships and a network of contacts at key operators, national bodies, and other relevant institutions. Over time you will gain specialist knowledge and develop the skills you require to progress your career at Wood Mackenzie.
Main Responsibilities

We are looking for an Analyst to work as part of our Research team covering Metals & Mining. We are particularly interested in hearing from people with experience in any of the following commodities – nickel, graphite, battery raw materials, or rare earth minerals.

You will have relevant experience, skills or knowledge that you can apply to the role, allowing you to contribute to our research from day one. Your existing knowledge will enhance our coverage as you develop your career.

Diversity is at the core of our business, and we welcome applications from a range of academic or professional backgrounds. Wood Mackenzie has adopted a hybrid approach, and we expect people to be present in our offices 2 days per week.
Your application should showcase your enthusiasm for the sectors we cover and demonstrate your ability in the following areas, which we require daily at Wood Mackenzie.

Research and data gathering – we identify and gather intelligence that feeds our industry expertise. We need inquisitive and enthusiastic people with a keen eye for detail. Conduct detailed research and analysis of graphite mining operations globally. Author regular publications providing our near-term and long-term outlook, focusing on pricing and demand analysis.

Analysis – we provide market-leading research using this data/intelligence and form opinions for and tell stories to our customers. We need people with natural curiosity with the potential to become experts in their field and with the ability to articulate – both verbally and in writing – clear, insightful analysis. Maintain and enhance proprietary data models and methodologies that underpin our graphite market analysis. Monitor market developments, regulatory changes, and technological trends impacting the industry.

Internal engagement – we collaborate with colleagues across the Wood Mackenzie business to deliver a compelling, integrated view of the commodities sector to customers through our innovative products. For this, we need people who are as comfortable working collaboratively as they are independently and who have excellent people skills.

External engagement – we proactively connect with our customers and contacts in the industries we serve, adding value and promoting the Wood Mackenzie brand. This requires people with the potential to become industry experts who have intellectual curiosity, passion, and excellent presentation skills to communicate with clients. Develop and maintain contacts with industry stakeholders to gather intelligence that will enrich the quality and timeliness of our research solution

Leadership/ Process Improvement – at Wood Mackenzie, we are committed to transforming the way we power our planet. The energy transition is here, and this impacts our customer base and how we serve them. We need colleagues with a change mindset, who are flexible, growth-orientated, and initiative-taking.
Preferred Skills & Experience
A degree in mining engineering, geology, economics, metallurgical engineering, or a related discipline.

2–6 years of relevant experience in the mining industry, consulting, or market analysis, ideally with exposure to any of the following commodities – nickel, graphite, battery raw materials or rare earth minerals.
Strong quantitative and qualitative research skills and good working knowledge of PowerPoint.
Excellent numerical analytic capabilities, including problem-solving and technical skills in Microsoft Excel.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English
additional languages are a plus.
The ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced, global environment.
Commitment to commercial excellence and experience managing clients.
A proactive and adaptable mindset, with a willingness to contribute to a variety of tasks and projects.
Expectations
Growth mindset - taking the initiative to learn new skills, seeking out feedback and actively applying what has been learned in the workplace.
Change agility - maintaining high performance through disruption and change.

We are a hybrid working company and the successful applicant will be expected to be physically present in the office at least 2 days per week to foster and contribute to a collaborative environment. This is subject to change per company policy.
This is a full-time role.
Diversity is at the core of our business
and we welcome applications from a range of academic or professional backgrounds.
Hear what our team has to say about working with us:
https://www.woodmac.com/careers/our-people/
Equal Opportunities

We are an equal opportunities employer. This means we are committed to recruiting the best people regardless of their race, colour, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability or protected veteran status. You can find out more about your rights under the law at
www.eeoc.gov
If you are applying for a role and have a physical or mental disability, we will support you with your application or through the hiring process.

Wood Mackenzie is the leading global provider of data and analytics solutions for the renewables, energy and natural resources sectors . head: Business Intelligence Engineer
info: 中国 广东省 深圳 · 1 周前 · 23 位申请者由招聘者推广 · 尚无可用回复洞察
job_details_content: ##              职位简介
Company Description
The QIMA story

At QIMA, we are on a mission to offer our clients smart solutions to make products you can trust. Operating in over 100 countries, we serve the consumer products, food, and life sciences industries and help more than 30,000 brands, retailers, manufacturers, and growers achieve quality excellence. We combine on-the-ground expertise with digital solutions that bring accuracy, transparency and intelligence for quality and compliance data. What sets us apart is our unique culture.

Our 6,000 Qimers live and make decisions every day by our QIMA Values. With client passion, integrity, and a commitment to making things simple, we disrupted the Testing, Inspection, and Certification industry. Are you ready to hop on this exciting ride with us and help us achieve our mission?
Job Description
The Job
Are you passionate about turning data into actionable insights that drive business success? We are seeking a skilled and motivated Business Intelligence Engineer to join our team.

In this role, you will leverage data to optimize internal operations, enhance business team efficiency, and empower our customers with critical information on their supply chain and quality. You will improve service quality across various aspects of supply chain management and quality assurance, driving our organization towards excellence.

If you love working with data to automate processes, solve problems, unlock new ways to analyse performance, and collaborate with cross-functional and international teams, we invite you to be a key player in our team's success!
Key Responsibilities

Data-driven Solutions: Work closely with business leaders to understand and identify user needs and develop data-driven solutions that optimize supply chain processes, enhance customer experiences, and improve operational efficiency.
Data Modelling and Automation: Design and implement scalable data models and automated processes to ensure efficient collection and integration of data from diverse sources.
Data Accuracy and Integrity: Ensure the data remains accurate over time by implementing data cleaning and validation procedures.
Collaboration with technical teams: Collaborate with the tech teams to enhance data collection processes.

Interactive Dashboards: Utilize Tableau Software to develop visually stunning and interactive dashboards tailored for both internal stakeholders and external clients, providing actionable insights to support decision-making.

Intuitive Interfaces: Collaborate with UI/UX teams and Data Visualization Lead to create intuitive interfaces that enhance user experience, leading to improved adoption and engagement with data-driven insights.
Performance Tuning: Continuously monitor and optimize dashboard performance to ensure swift responsiveness, providing users with seamless access to real-time insights.

Data Analysis and Process Improvement: Leverage data analysis to identify opportunities for process improvement and contribute to strategic projects aimed at enhancing business performance and competitiveness.
Qualifications
The Candidate
Candidate Requirements

Education and Experience: You have master's degree in a relevant field (e.g., Supply Chain, Operations Management, Data Analytics) and have at least 3+ years of experience in a similar role, working with BI solutions such as Tableau, Power BI, Alteryx, Qlik Sense, etc.
Analytical Skills: Excellent analytical skills, with the ability to interpret complex datasets and derive actionable insights to drive business decisions.

Lean Enthusiast: You demonstrate a genuine passion for lean practices and process optimization, with a proven track record of driving efficiency improvements and cost savings through lean methodologies.
Data Modelling & ETL: You are able design robust data models that facilitate efficient data integration and ensure data integrity and consistency across the organization's systems and processes.
SQL and Database Skills: You are proficient in writing SQL queries and working with relational databases to manipulate and analyze data effectively.
Programming Skills: You are familiar with Python scripting for data manipulation and automation, enabling efficient data processing and analysis workflows.

Communication Skills: You demonstrate excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to effectively collaborate with cross-functional teams and stakeholders to drive data-driven initiatives forward.
Additional Information
Preferred Skills (not mandatory):

Quality Management: You possess experience in quality management, with expertise in either laboratory testing processes, supply chain dynamics, or quality assurance. You also have a good understanding of the challenges and opportunities in those domains.
Data Visualization: You are familiar with data visualization best practices, and love creating compelling and informative visualizations.
Portfolio: You have a portfolio of existing BI projects to showcase your expertise and creativity in data visualization practices.
If you're excited about leveraging data to drive meaningful change and want to be part of a dynamic team committed to innovation and excellence, we'd love to hear from you!
Please send your CV to brian.cheung@qima.com.

1. Data-driven Solutions: Work closely with business leaders to understand and identify user needs and develop data-driven solutions that optimize supply chain processes, enhance customer experiences, and improve operational efficiency.
2. Data Modelling and Automation: Design and implement scalable data models and automated processes to ensure efficient collection and integration of data from diverse sources.
3. Data Accuracy and Integrity: Ensure the data remains accurate over time by implementing data cleaning and validation procedures.
4. Collaboration with technical teams: Collaborate with the tech teams to enhance data collection processes.

5. Interactive Dashboards: Utilize Tableau Software to develop visually stunning and interactive dashboards tailored for both internal stakeholders and external clients, providing actionable insights to support decision-making.

6. Intuitive Interfaces: Collaborate with UI/UX teams and Data Visualization Lead to create intuitive interfaces that enhance user experience, leading to improved adoption and engagement with data-driven insights.
7. Performance Tuning: Continuously monitor and optimize dashboard performance to ensure swift responsiveness, providing users with seamless access to real-time insights.

8. Data Analysis and Process Improvement: Leverage data analysis to identify opportunities for process improvement and contribute to strategic projects aimed at enhancing business performance and competitiveness.

1. Education and Experience: You have master's degree in a relevant field (e.g., Supply Chain, Operations Management, Data Analytics) and have at least 3+ years of experience in a similar role, working with BI solutions such as Tableau, Power BI, Alteryx, Qlik Sense, etc.
2. Analytical Skills: Excellent analytical skills, with the ability to interpret complex datasets and derive actionable insights to drive business decisions.

3. Lean Enthusiast: You demonstrate a genuine passion for lean practices and process optimization, with a proven track record of driving efficiency improvements and cost savings through lean methodologies.
4. Data Modelling & ETL: You are able design robust data models that facilitate efficient data integration and ensure data integrity and consistency across the organization's systems and processes.
5. SQL and Database Skills: You are proficient in writing SQL queries and working with relational databases to manipulate and analyze data effectively.
6. Programming Skills: You are familiar with Python scripting for data manipulation and automation, enabling efficient data processing and analysis workflows.

7. Communication Skills: You demonstrate excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to effectively collaborate with cross-functional teams and stakeholders to drive data-driven initiatives forward.

1. Quality Management: You possess experience in quality management, with expertise in either laboratory testing processes, supply chain dynamics, or quality assurance. You also have a good understanding of the challenges and opportunities in those domains.
2. Data Visualization: You are familiar with data visualization best practices, and love creating compelling and informative visualizations.
3. Portfolio: You have a portfolio of existing BI projects to showcase your expertise and creativity in data visualization practices.

head: Analyst, Aluminium Assets
info: 中国 北京市 · 1 周前 · 28 位会员点击过“申请”由招聘者推广 ·
job_details_content: ##              职位简介
About CRU:

CRU International is a leading provider of business intelligence and consulting services in the metals, mining, and fertilizer industries. With over 50 years of experience, we offer valuable insights and analysis that help our clients make informed decisions in an ever-changing global market.
About the role:

CRU seeks an Aluminium Assets Analyst to deliver asset-focused subscription services and convert complex datasets into clear, client-ready insight. The role will maintain aluminium asset models and datasets, conduct primary and secondary research with industry contacts, and translate findings into market-shaping analysis and timely service deliverables. The analyst will support Service Leads, collaborate across the Assets and broader analysis teams to ensure consistent, high-quality delivery, and help identify commercial opportunities to grow the service.
Key Responsibilities:
1. Build and maintain a strong understanding of aluminium commodity markets and the assets within remit
2. Contribute to asset-level analysis and modelling for the Service(s) under remit
3. Maintain, organise and update the data series and aluminium asset models that form part of customer deliverables
4. Conduct primary and secondary research, including developing and managing relationships with market, industry and institutional contacts
5. Translate research and datasets into clear, market-shaping analysis and timely client deliverables
6. Attend industry conferences and events to build networks and represent CRU
7. Support client-facing activities including meetings, demos and webinars
8. Respond to internal requests for data and asset-related information and help identify commercial opportunities to grow the service
Qualifications:
1. Essential: Bachelor’s degree from a top-tier institution
2. Preferred: Further education or certification in a subject relevant to commodity research (e.g., geology, economics, engineering)
Skills & Experience:
1. Strong ability to gather and critically analyse both “soft” (qualitative) and “hard” (quantitative) data to identify trends and drivers and generate actionable insights
2. Highly numerate with experience interpreting, manipulating and managing large datasets and understanding relevant mathematical concepts and processes
3. Educational background in a quantitative subject
4. Experience conducting primary and secondary research and engaging with industry contacts
5. Able to collaborate across global analysis teams, share best practice, and balance personal initiative with team-based working
6. Strong verbal and written communication skills for client deliverables and presentations
What We Offer:
1. Competitive salary and flexible benefits package.
2. Opportunities for professional growth and development as part of a global company.
3. A collaborative and supportive work environment.
4. The chance to work with industry-leading experts and over a diverse range of topics and projects.
company_box_content: Commodity markets are more dynamic and complex than ever, shaped by volatility, new regulations, and rapid technological change. At CRU, we empower our clients to navigate this landscape with clarity and confidence—delivering trusted, independent data and insights with speed and precision.

Since 1969, CRU has been a global leader in business intelligence for the metals, mining, and fertilizer industries. Our expertise spans market analysis, commodity data and price assessments, strategic consultancy, and vibrant industry communities and events. With over 450 experts worldwide—including teams on the ground in China—we combine robust primary research with deep local knowledge to help you stay ahead in fast-moving markets.

Our acquisition of Exawatt strengthens our capabilities in electrification technologies, from renewable power generation to solar PV and energy storage. As industries accelerate their drive to decarbonise, CRU delivers meaningful data and insight to support your sustainability journey.

We’ve unified our brand to make it simpler and more intuitive to engage with CRU—whether you’re accessing our data platforms, joining our community events, or partnering with our consulting teams. In a world of constant change, CRU is committed to propelling your success forward—deciphering market trends, shaping sustainable strategies, and empowering you every step of the way.
company_description: CRU International is a leading provider of business intelligence and consulting for metals, mining, and fertilizer industries, with over 50 years of experience and 450+ experts globally. It offers market analysis, commodity data, price assessments, and strategic consultancy, helping clients navigate dynamic markets and sustainability challenges.

head: Assoc 2, Statistics
info: 中国 北京市 北京市 · 1 周前 · 8 位会员点击过“申请”由招聘者推广 ·
job_details_content: ##              职位简介
Job Overview
Provide applied statistical methodology support and enhancement to a wide range of syndicated data offerings.
Essential Responsibility
Own the maintenance of existing statistical methodologies and ensure quality control of related offerings.
Proactively monitor, conduct quality control (QC), and implement procedures to maintain stable and accurate data trends.
Develop and utilize various data analysis methods across multiple platforms, employing diverse data tools to build and implement efficient techniques, QC reports, or research new methodologies.
Assist in the test-case design and validation of new methodologies.
Conduct statistical programming, develop, and apply statistical QC and summarization to meet internal information needs.
Perform data reviews to derive conclusions from analyses, write up results, and deliver regular summary reports for core processes.
Ensure comprehensive and organized project documentation and file management.

Communicate effectively with cross-functional teams to illustrate methodology design. Collaborate extensively with these teams to implement new methodologies, report progress, and meet critical deadlines.
Work in a Big Data environment, rewriting code using SAS, SQL, Python, R, or other languages as needed.
Qualifications
Master’s degree or equivalent in Statistics, Mathematics, Data Science, Computer Science, or a related quantitative science field such as operations research, market research, or applied statistics.
2-3 years of industry experience in statistical methodology and AI/ML.
Solid statistical knowledge, including regression, hypothesis testing, sampling statistics (universe, sample design, projection factors), and predictive modeling.
Practical experience using AI/ML for imputation, projection, and outlier detection is preferred.
Strong logical thinking and programming skills in Python and PySpark are required.
Applied experience in SAS and/or other statistical programming is preferred.
Technical ability to extract, manipulate, and combine datasets from different sources and platforms. Hands-on experience with Snowflake is a plus.
Strong written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to explain concepts to both statistical and non-statistical audiences. Be proficient in English communication.
Good time management and learning ability, can deliver good results under pressure.

head: Model Governance Analyst
info: 中国 上海市 上海市 · 重新发布时间: 4 天前 · 26 位会员点击过“申请”由招聘者推广 ·
job_details_content: ##              职位简介
Introduction

Since 1973, East West Bank has served as a pathway to success. With over 110 locations across the U.S. and Asia, we are the premier financial bridge between the East and West. Our teams of experienced, multi-cultural professionals help guide businesses and community members on both sides of the Pacific looking to explore new markets and create new opportunities, and our sustained growth and expertise in industries like real estate, entertainment and media, private equity and venture capital, and high-tech help build sustainable businesses and expand our associates’ potential for career advancement.

Headquartered in California, East West Bank (Nasdaq: EWBC) is a top-performing commercial bank with a strong foundation, an enterprising spirit and a commitment to absolute integrity. East West Bank gives people the confidence to reach further.
Overview

Model Governance Analyst plays an essential role to the model lifecycle, with key responsibilities including the authoring of high-quality technical documentation, supporting model development, and managing model performance monitoring processes. This role will collaborate closely with model developers and key stakeholders, including Model Risk Management (MRM).

Key responsibilities include clearly translating complex mathematical, statistical, and machine learning concepts into auditable documentation, guiding model developers in fulfilling model risk requirements, maintaining the Model Ongoing Performance Monitoring (OPM) reports, and ensuring the timely remediation of documentation-related findings from validators, auditors, and MRM.
Responsibilities

Authoring and maintaining comprehensive documentation for key Treasury models (e.g., liquidity risk, market risk), ensuring compliance with internal Model Risk Management policies and regulatory standards.
Leading MRM-related documentation activities, including the timely remediation of validator findings, and effectively bridging the gap between technical model developers and MRM’s requirements.
Responsible for Model Ongoing Performance Monitoring (OPM) processes and reports.

Managing the end-to-end documentation lifecycle for models, including maintaining standardized templates, version control, model change log, and coordinating review cycles with stakeholders (e.g., model owners and Model Risk teams).
Identifying, researching, and proposing improvements to documentation processes, templates, and governance procedures to enhance quality and operational efficiency.
Performing other financial analysis, research assignments, and other job duties as assigned.
Qualifications

5+ years of proven experience in a quantitative role within financial industry, with a direct focus on model documentation, model validation, or model development. Experience specifically with Treasury models (e.g., Liquidity Risk, Interest Rate Risk) is a significant advantage.
Bachelor’s degree required, preferably in a quantitative field (e.g., Economics, Finance, Mathematics, Statistics, Actuarial Science, Data Science).
An advanced degree in a quantitative field and/or a professional certification directly related to financial risk management (such as CFA, FRM) is a strong plus.
Exceptional technical writing and communication skills in English.
Demonstrable understanding of financial products and the core principles of market risk, liquidity risk, and/or broader risk management.
Proficiency with Microsoft Office suite is required.
Meticulous attention to detail and a rigorous, analytical approach to problem-solving.
Experience with statistical software (e.g., R, Python) or data tools (e.g., VBA, Power BI, SQL) is preferred.
Strong project management skills, with the ability to manage the documentation lifecycle for multiple models simultaneously and meet strict deadlines.
Excellent interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively with quantitative model developers, validators, and other stakeholders .

2.  Leading MRM-related documentation activities, including the timely remediation of validator findings, and effectively bridging the gap between technical model developers and MRM’s requirements.
3.  Responsible for Model Ongoing Performance Monitoring (OPM) processes and reports.

head: 商业化营销数据分析师（数据工程/DE方向）-Global Business Solutions
info: 中国 上海市 上海市 · 3 周前 · 23 位申请者由招聘者推广 · 积极审核申请者
job_details_content: ##              职位简介
职位描述
1、负责商业化营销业务的数据架构设计与管理，整合多方数据源，搭建和维护数据管道及数据库，提高数据查询效率、可用性和准确性，为营销度量和策略分析提供高质量数据支持；
2、推进数据工程与分析方法的创新应用，包括自动化工具建设与数据资产优化，降低找数、用数门槛，提升数据可用性与分析效率；
3、建立并维护营销指标体系和报表体系，确保策略分析和业务决策所需数据能够及时、准确地获取；
4、执行业务驱动的数据分析项目，将复杂业务问题抽象为数据问题，开展数据建模、验证与优化，支持业务团队进行洞察与决策；
5、管理数据项目进展，合理安排任务优先级与交付时间，确保项目按时、高质量完成。
职位要求
1、本科及以上学历，统计学、数据科学、计算机科学、应用数学、信息技术或相关专业背景，硕士优先；
2、3年以上数据工程、数据分析或数据科学相关工作经验；具备营销数据分析、互联网业务或跨国团队协作经验者优先；
3、精通SQL，熟悉主流大数据技术栈（如Hadoop、Hive、Spark、Flink等），具备数据仓库实施、ETL开发及基于API的数据集成和自动化能力；
4、熟悉数据建模、数据治理和BI可视化工具（如Tableau、Power BI等）；具备统计分析、机器学习或因果推断经验者优先；
5、能快速理解复杂业务场景，将业务问题转化为数据问题，具备扎实的数据工程能力和量化分析思维；
6、优秀的中英文沟通能力，能与跨职能、跨地区团队高效协作；具备较强的自驱力、逻辑思维能力与持续创新和优化意识。
About Us

Founded in 2012, ByteDance's mission is to inspire creativity and enrich life. With a suite of more than a dozen products, including TikTok, Lemon8, CapCut and Pico as well as platforms specific to the China market, including Toutiao, Douyin, and Xigua, ByteDance has made it easier and more fun for people to connect with, consume, and create content.
Why Join ByteDance

Inspiring creativity is at the core of ByteDance's mission. Our innovative products are built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and enrich life - a mission we work towards every day.

As ByteDancers, we strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our Company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.
Diversity & Inclusion

ByteDance is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At ByteDance, our mission is to inspire creativity and enrich life. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.
company_box_content: ByteDance is a global incubator of platforms at the cutting edge of commerce, content, entertainment and enterprise services - over 2.5bn people interact with ByteDance products including TikTok.
Creation is the core of ByteDance's purpose. Our products are built to help imaginations thrive. This is doubly true of the teams that make our innovations possible.

Together, we inspire creativity and enrich life - a mission we aim towards achieving every day. At ByteDance, we create together and grow together. That's how we drive impact - for ourselves, our company, and the users we serve. We are committed to building a safe, healthy and positive online environment for all our users.
We have over 110,000 employees based in more than 30 countries globally. Join us.
company_description: ByteDance is a global technology company with over 110,000 employees, known for products like TikTok. The job involves data architecture design, ETL development, data pipeline management, and big data technologies like Hadoop and Spark, core to data engineering responsibilities.

1. At least 2 years of analytical work experience in the gaming, tech or a data-driven industry, with a proven track record working in close connection with product teams.

4. Working proficiency in both English and Chinese.

Have advanced skills in aligning financial strategy with operational execution and cross-regional financial coordination
5+years of experience in product finance controlling.
You are highly skilled in financial planning & analysis, cost control methodologies, global financial reporting standards, and data-driven decision-making tools.
Exceptional PPT skills with expertise in creating visually compelling, logically structured, and data-driven presentations that effectively convey complex information to diverse audiences.
Passionate about cross-functional collaboration and data-driven problem solving
Innovative around streamlining financial processes and aligning financial strategy with business growth objectives.
Degree in Accounting or a related field (e.g. Finance or Science)
Be strength in logical thinking and a structured approach in problem solving, with ability of breaking down complex financial challenges into actionable solutions.
Fluent in English

At ABB, we help industries outrun - leaner and cleaner. Here, progress is an expectation - for you, your team, and the world. As a global market leader, we’ll give you what you need to make it happen. It won’t always be easy, growing takes grit. But at ABB, you’ll never run alone. Run what runs the world

This role sits within ABB’s Robotics business, a leading global robotics company. We’re entering an exciting new chapter as we’ve announced the plan for SoftBank Group to acquire ABB Robotics. SoftBank is a globally recognized technology group and investor/operator focused on AI, robotics, and next-generation computing. By joining us now, you’ll be part of a pioneering team shaping the future of robotics—working alongside world-class experts in a fast-moving, innovation-driven environment.
We value people from different backgrounds. Could this be your story? Apply today or visit www.abb.com to read more about us and learn about the impact of our solutions across the globe.
1. Supporting the preparation of the relevant end to end financial reporting, business planning, budgeting, and forecasting.

3. Working with management team to understand and analyze drivers of financial performance and identifying market trends.

4. Preparing financial modeling for various hypotheses and analyzing impact to the organization and validating the accuracy of financial data and business information and reporting through reconciliation and review of exceptions.

1. Experience with power BI for data visualization and dashboard creation is a valuable plus, enhancing your ability to present complex financial trends to stakeholders effectively.

head: BNEF Carbon Capture and Storage Data Analyst (6-Month Contract)
info: 中国 上海市 上海市 · 2 周前 · 20 位会员点击过“申请”由招聘者推广 ·
job_details_content: ##              职位简介
Location
Shanghai
Business Area
Research
Ref #
Description & Requirements

BloombergNEF (BNEF) is a strategic research provider covering global commodity markets and the disruptive technologies driving the transition to a low-carbon economy. We help our clients gain a clear perspective on the trends in technology, finance, policy and economics that are driving transformation across the energy value chain. Our expert coverage assesses pathways for the power, transport, industry, buildings and agriculture sectors to adapt to the energy transition. We help commodity trading, corporate strategy, finance and policy professionals navigate change and generate opportunities. BNEF has 300 staff based in 20 offices across the globe.

To succeed at BNEF we need people who can work in an independent manner and show initiative to develop their own viewpoints, yet be collaborative with colleagues. We don't rely on the status quo, we look for innovative yet pragmatic thinking that turns big ideas into real insights and impact. Working at BNEF sometimes feels chaotic
we need teams that are both dynamic and structured to generate the unique perspectives our clients really value. Our teams are diverse, creative, focused, and fun!

We are looking for a highly motivated, talented individual to join our Sustainable Materials analyst team to undertake research and data analysis on the carbon capture and storage sector. You will be based in our Beijing/ Shanghai office and working as part of a global research team at BNEF – the world's leading independent provider of analysis, tools, data and research to decision makers leading the transition to a low-carbon economy. Our data-driven research helps clients stay on top of developments across the materials, automotive and energy industries, supported by the unique depth and breadth of the Bloomberg Terminal and bnef.com platforms.
We'll Trust You To
Conduct research and track company and government activities and projects that look to deploy carbon capture, utilization and storage (CCUS) to reduce emissions

Gather and organize data sets pertaining to project announcements, partnerships, technology supply agreements, financing arrangements and other aspects that are critical for clients to assess project feasibility

Collaborate with other colleagues from across BNEF and Bloomberg to create well-structured and organized databases that are then visualized and made available as tools, databases and other publications for clients to understand the growth of the sector and identify opportunities
Liaise with companies, industry bodies and government entities to achieve the highest quality of data for the various tools and publications
Identify and work with third-party data providers and vendors to improve the completeness and accuracy of BNEF’s data products within the Sustainable Materials team
Address client questions in a timely manner and present the insights and trends to clients and prospective clients at internal and external events and conferences
You'll Need To Have
Demonstrated interest and enthusiasm for the carbon capture and storage sector
Excellent critical thinking and strong quantitative skills
Proficiency in English and Mandarin, and good communication skills: writing (for research projects and databases in English) and speaking (for presentations)
Excellent working knowledge of MS Office (Excel, Word and PowerPoint)
Ability to derive information from public sources and in person / telephone interviews
Ability to act as a self-starter, have a high work rate and work on parallel tasks with attention to detail
A collaborative attitude and ability to work closely with other teams at BNEF, including our Data, Hydrogen, Oil and Metals teams
We'd Love To See
Graduate degree in sciences, engineering or data sciences
Knowledge of SQL and Python
Experience in analyzing the energy, industry or carbon capture sectors
If This Sounds Like You
Apply if you think we're a good match. We'll get in touch to let you know what the next steps are, but in the meantime feel free to have a look at this:
Contract will be administered by third-party vendor as a dispatched worker arrangement with Bloomberg.
Discover what makes Bloomberg unique - watch our for an inside look at our culture, values, and the people behind our success.
1. Conduct research and track company and government activities and projects that look to deploy carbon capture, utilization and storage (CCUS) to reduce emissions

2. Gather and organize data sets pertaining to project announcements, partnerships, technology supply agreements, financing arrangements and other aspects that are critical for clients to assess project feasibility

3. Collaborate with other colleagues from across BNEF and Bloomberg to create well-structured and organized databases that are then visualized and made available as tools, databases and other publications for clients to understand the growth of the sector and identify opportunities
4. Liaise with companies, industry bodies and government entities to achieve the highest quality of data for the various tools and publications
5. Identify and work with third-party data providers and vendors to improve the completeness and accuracy of BNEF’s data products within the Sustainable Materials team
6. Address client questions in a timely manner and present the insights and trends to clients and prospective clients at internal and external events and conferences
1. Demonstrated interest and enthusiasm for the carbon capture and storage sector
2. Excellent critical thinking and strong quantitative skills
3. Proficiency in English and Mandarin, and good communication skills: writing (for research projects and databases in English) and speaking (for presentations)
4. Excellent working knowledge of MS Office (Excel, Word and PowerPoint)
5. Ability to derive information from public sources and in person / telephone interviews
6. Ability to act as a self-starter, have a high work rate and work on parallel tasks with attention to detail
7. A collaborative attitude and ability to work closely with other teams at BNEF, including our Data, Hydrogen, Oil and Metals teams
1. Graduate degree in sciences, engineering or data sciences
2. Knowledge of SQL and Python
3. Experience in analyzing the energy, industry or carbon capture sectors
company_box_content: Bloomberg is a global leader in business and financial information, delivering trusted data, news, and insights that bring transparency and efficiency, and fairness to markets. We help connect influential communities across the global financial ecosystem via reliable technology solutions that enable our customers to make more informed decisions and foster better collaboration.

We challenge the status quo through constant innovation. We collaborate broadly because we know that other perspectives matter. We put our customers first, as a guiding beacon. And we believe doing the right thing – by our people, our clients, and our communities – is the best thing for our business.
company_description: Bloomberg is a global leader in business and financial information with a large workforce. The job involves research, data gathering, and analysis for carbon capture projects, using tools like Excel and potentially SQL/Python, focusing on insights and reporting typical of data analysis.
